atoms.atom_name: Year-over-year margin bridge with residual reconciliation
notes: 2026-07-10 DISCOVER finding: the report renders TWO adjacent margin-bridge sections (annual_report.py's Phase BE ledger-based + Phase NT years[]-based) whose gross-margin deltas disagree for the same year transition (e.g. 2016-2017: +116,919 vs +116,417), unlabelled -- a live Expert-Hour-failing legibility+consistency gap, same root cause as the E2 two-pipelines finding, not yet fixed. External research LANDED: price/volume/mix split is a real, standard FP&A convention (Corporate Finance Institute) but NOT shown to be energy-retail-specific; Centrica plc's real FY2025 results instead use sector-specific named drivers (weather, commodity-curve shape, regulatory reconciliations, bad debt quantified directly by the CFO) much closer to this bridge's existing driver set -- VALIDATES the current design over a generic price/volume/mix rebuild. Customer count as informational-only (not a £ driver) is ALSO validated -- Centrica discloses customer numbers as a standalone KPI, not inside its £ bridge. Ofgem/CMA's own methodology uses levels-and-trends, not a bridge/waterfall convention at all -- a genuine, useful negative finding. Remaining real gap is the two-pipelines disagreement above, not the driver set. DEPENDENCY FORMALIZED 2026-07-10 (fifteenth draw, same treatment just applied to E2_revenue_reconciliation): the two-pipelines root cause is Epoch-2 core per the director-closed POINT_IN_TIME_SNAPSHOT_TIER1.md gate -- sequencing belongs to the advisor's epoch framing, not a B1-owned FRAME action. loop_stage set to idle, depends_on added.

atoms.atom_name: Opex/cost-to-serve mechanism -- full B2 taxonomy (true/benchmark ledger, fixed floor, break-even, segment capital/ROCE, concentration)
notes: 2026-07-10 v1 (parts a/b/c) as before. 2026-07-10 v2 BUILT (director-direct NTFY, B2_OPEX_TAXONOMY_EXPANSION.md): category (4) infrastructure-at-commercial-rates (cloud/market-data/BSC-REC-SEC/Bacs, all estimate-flagged, docs/market_research/B2_CATEGORY4_INFRASTRUCTURE_ANCHORS.md) + category (5) fixed governance/professional floor (audit/legal/Ofgem-fee/insurance/cosec, own P&L line, golive-conditional tagging, docs/market_research/B2_CATEGORY5_GOVERNANCE_ANCHORS.md) + category (6) fixed/stepped/variable classification + CAC per channel/segment (PCS one-off residential real £50-60/dual-fuel, broker ongoing per-kWh I&C real 0.2-2.0p/kWh, docs/market_research/B2_CATEGORY6_CAC_ANCHORS.md) -- all built into saas/opex_ledger.py. Break-even analysis (emergent, book size/mix vs the fixed floor, recomputed each run, R12-compliant) built + rendered. Segment capital-employed (real per-segment AR + revenue-share-allocated hedging-capital-cost proxy for collateral/exposure) + ROCE + persistent-under-hurdle decision-artefact mechanism built in company/finance/segment_capital.py. Single-customer gross-margin concentration wired for real into the previously-dead company/risk/concentration_risk.py (added a metric field + gross-margin builder/checker, distinct from its original revenue-share design). Self-caught a real wiring bug live: segment working-capital (bare segment grain from billing_ledger.json) vs segment revenue-share (finer 'segment commodity' grain from segment_split) would have silently mixed two incompatible granularities in one dict -- fixed by aggregating both to bare-segment before combining. 78 new tests (24 opex_ledger + 8 concentration_risk + 16 segment_capital + hooks/other), 16,601 tests collected (full suite), epistemic PASS, verified live in-browser (screenshot, zero console errors, zero NaN/undefined) on the real Supplier > Financial tab. 2026-07-10 v3 (level 2->3, director numbers WIRED, from_rich_20260710_190908.md): ROCE hurdle 12% pre-tax + concentration limit 15%/amber-10% are now real, live thresholds (were None/not-set) -- real breaches confirmed live: SME + resi under the ROCE hurdle, single-customer concentration a genuine RED breach (C_IC1 29.15% of gross margin), exactly the standing risk exception the director expected, rendered not hidden. concentration_risk.py gained a 3-tier (green/amber/red) status. Break-even (5.1 customers) explicitly flagged provisional=true + a whale-distortion caveat per his instruction, pending segment-level re-derivation after MARGIN_REALISM steps 4-5 (not this atom's scope). 9 new/updated tests, 16,614 tests collected, epistemic PASS, verified live. Level bumped to target: the full B2 taxonomy's ONLY remaining open number is B2(b)'s AI-compute costing basis/rate -- a separate, pre-existing open question tracked on its own, not blocking this atom's own scope. loop_stage -> harden.

atoms.atom_name: Hedge-tariff alignment (cost locked when price is locked)
notes: 2026-07-10 DISCOVER finding, verified not assumed: MARGIN_REALISM Step 4's goal is substantially already built. Fixed/pass-through tariffs: decide_hedge_fraction() decided once per term (volatility as-of term start), held constant until the next renewal -- confirmed via real 2020 data (start_hf == avg_hf exactly for the large majority of customers). Deemed (out-of-contract/SVT-adjacent) tariffs: spot+premium, explicitly NO forward hedge -- correctly matches SVT tracking the cap's own wholesale-observation window. Flex tariffs: weekly re-hedge at rolling reference price -- matches a short-reset tariff's own commitment horizon. One honest exception NOT glossed over: a few I&C customers show hf changing within a calendar year, plausibly multiple genuine contract renewals (I&C terms are often 3-6 months in reality) rather than a mid-term re-decision bug -- NOT independently re-verified against the raw per-term log (not retained in the report-extracted shape), flagged as resting on a plausible inference, not a full re-derivation.

2026-07-11 HARDEN-sweep gap CLOSED (commit 8dc14ca1): hedge_fraction was always on every settlement record but never reached a business surface (harden_sweep:live_site:B3_hedge_tariff_alignment). Added consumption-weighted avg_hedge_fraction to per_customer_lifetime (saas/reporting/annual_report.py) and threaded it into each per-customer site JSON (tools/generate_customer_data.py) plus a new visible 'Avg. Hedge Coverage' row on the customer portal account card (site/customers/index.html, right after Cost to Serve). 5 new tests (weighted-average math, None-when-zero-consumption, full JSON thread), browser-verified the row renders correctly (Playwright, injected test value, reverted after). Live data awaits the next natural sim run -- the field wasn't in run_output_latest.json generated before this fix landed.

atoms.atom_name: Customer segment flavour fields (smart meter, dual-fuel, home/business type)
notes: 2026-07-10: found engagement_level (simulation/household_segments.py, drives active/passive renewal for EVERY segment in run_phase2b.py, not resi-gated) was computed but never surfaced anywhere -- a dangling comment ('same caveat as engagement_level above') referenced it as if already shown, but it wasn't in this file at all. Fixed: added to tools/generate_customer_sample.py + a new SIM tab column, verified against real production data (dual-fuel legs share the same level, I&C customers get one too). 4 new tests.

2026-07-11 M3 DISCOVER+FRAME (docs/design/M3_DISCOVER_FRAME.md): confirmed Q4/Q5/Q6 gaps still live (portal still reads saas.customers.CUSTOMERS directly; property_model.py generator still unanchored; CUSTOMERS still a fixed 31-entry list, single fixed payment-rng seed, zero population-draw code found). New finding: two company-side onboarding/acquisition-journey trackers (company/crm/acquisition_journey.py, company/crm/onboarding_journey.py) are real, SLC-anchored, but completely unwired (zero non-test callers) -- same 'paper compliance' class M2 found in payments. BUILD deferred on all three M3 sub-components pending director/advisor sequencing; one low-risk item flagged as independently actionable (onboarding_journey.py SLC-deadline wiring).

atoms.atom_name: Hidden adoption physics (bother-threshold, friction sensitivity, trust/reassurance, reward responsiveness)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P3, advisor-staged, director-decided -- ADOPTION_JOURNEY_REGISTER.md, do not start): ToU/control/DER adoption is a journey not a switch, and is the missing precondition for ToU tariffs meaning anything behaviourally. Each customer carries hidden traits (being-bothered threshold -- for many, the real £ at stake sits BELOW it, must be honestly true in the population, never tuned away; friction sensitivity; trust/reassurance needs; reward responsiveness), extending the engagement archetypes and consuming the deferred NUDGE_PHYSICS mechanisms (present bias, friction costs, defaults, social norms, commitment devices) with the same behavioural-science anchoring discipline as the shipped framing/tone work. Non-negotiable (Law A): adoption rate is a DIAGNOSTIC, never a target -- no tuning hidden susceptibility to hit a number. Advisor amendment (director-approved, Law B): susceptibility DISTRIBUTIONS are BASELINE-world fidelity, anchored to real ToU/smart-tariff uptake data, never tuned; the INCENTIVE MECHANICS the company offers are supplier-side strategy it must learn -- that line stays clean. The wall holds: the company observes offers/responses/behaviour, never the hidden thresholds. PLACEMENT: Epoch 4 -- dependencies unbuilt or in flight (C customer psychology partial, W2 population draw L0, M1 event stream in flight); building this on a fixed cast under single-pass truth would be mechanically-real but behaviourally-hollow, the same trap that correctly deferred RY. Open questions left open until the epoch opens: anchor sources for adoption/threshold distributions, how journey progress is measured company-side.

atoms.atom_name: Key moments as conversion windows (EV purchase, house move, boiler failure, smart-meter install, bill shock, renewal)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P3, advisor-staged, director-decided -- ADOPTION_JOURNEY_REGISTER.md, do not start): adoption is state- and event-dependent -- these are events in the SIM's own event stream; the company's job is DETECTING/INFERRING the moment through the wall and acting within the window, never reading the SIM's ground truth directly. PLACEMENT: Epoch 4, same dependency block as C4_adoption_physics (C customer psychology partial, W2 population draw L0, M1 event stream in flight) -- do not build ahead of those. Non-negotiable: nudge mechanics stay two-sided under Consumer Duty -- gamified incentives and pressure at key moments have mis-selling/vulnerability edges the compliance organs must see.

atoms.atom_name: Three settlement clocks (physical/financial/regulatory) reconciled per bill
notes: 2026-07-10: real evidence feeding this atom found while tracing E2's two-pipelines root cause -- saas/bill_generator.py bills non-commodity cost via a single blended £/MWh rate (saas/non_commodity.py) while simulation/hedged_settlement.py computes the same real-world cost per-levy, per-settlement-period (RO/CfD/CCL/CM/FiT + DUoS/TNUoS individually). The resulting gap between what's billed and what's settled is bidirectional/non-monotonic across years (+27.7% to -25.3%), consistent with a genuine volume/timing mismatch this atom's own scope names, not a simple missing-component bug -- this is the concrete first reconciliation target when this atom is built. PLACEMENT CORRECTION (director, closing the sibling POINT_IN_TIME_SNAPSHOT_TIER1.md gate): this atom is Epoch-2 CORE -- the BILLING-side face of the reveal-over-time spine, sibling to W1_reveal_over_time (the COMPANY-side/pricing-decision face), one architecture not two. loop_stage set to idle to match: sequencing is the advisor's epoch framing to set, not a FRAME-stage design task to pick up independently right now. Lane charter written 2026-07-10 (docs/design/charters/D_billing_metering.md, dial reached 4/hot, charter earned per the map's own rule) as part of the same director-approved bounded start that built W1's shared spine foundation -- explicitly registers this lane's own thinking (sub-capability tree, L2/L3/L4 definitions, roadmap) WITHOUT claiming any reconciliation code has been built; loop_stage correctly stays idle, this atom's own build still awaits the advisor's epoch framing naming its turn.

atoms.atom_name: Estimated billing & catch-up rebilling cycle
notes: 2026-07-11 UPGRADED FROM THEORETICAL TO QUANTIFIED (director/advisor decision, ADVISOR_ACK_ESTIMATE_GAP.md + ADVISOR_STEER_ESTIMATION_GAP.md, answering the bill-arithmetic fix's own residual finding): surfaced while fixing the reads-vs-usage rounding defect (ADVISOR_STEER_BILL_ARITHMETIC.md), a full historical sweep found 425 of 1588 rendered bills (~27%) are Estimated-read bills whose CHARGED AMOUNT is computed from true (SIM ground-truth) consumption rather than the estimate -- the billing engine has no estimate-then-true-up model at all today. Confirmed NOT a new epistemic-wall leak: simulation/meter_reads.py generates the actual/estimated read-status narrative from ALREADY-COMPUTED bills, so no company-side decision ever chose true consumption over an estimate -- the choice was simply never modeled. Director's decision: this is NOT a standalone patch -- it IS this atom, and the epoch exists to fix exactly this class. Sequence per THE_VALUE_CYCLE_FRAMING (unchanged, not resequenced by this finding): rails sim landed -> estimated billing on EAC/profile -> actual-read catch-up rebilling within back-billing law -> settlement timetable -> DD cash engine. The mechanism ships forward-looking first; the 425 historical bills are rederived through it as part of M4's full-history replay -- they already sit under the PROVISIONAL banner, no emergency relabel needed. level_current STAYS at 0 (not yet built, still framed) -- this is a quantification-of-magnitude upgrade, not a build-progress one.

atoms.atom_name: Incentives and loyalty integrated into billing (points, rewards, free/half-price hours, miles, prizes as bill-visible artefacts)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P3, advisor-staged, director-decided -- ADOPTION_JOURNEY_REGISTER.md, do not start): ToU/EV loyalty mechanics (points, rewards, free hours, half-price hours, miles, prizes) are becoming standard industry practice and are bill-visible artefacts (statement lines, reward balances, discounted-window tariff structures) -- NOT marketing copy. They touch the billing lane and the bill artefact standard directly, and their cost is a real P&L line. Where flexibility value per customer sits below the bother threshold (see C4_adoption_physics), engagement/loyalty mechanics carry the persuasion load, and the company must DISCOVER that rather than assume rational £-response. Advisor placement decision: loyalty economics live INSIDE the billing + marketing lanes -- explicitly no new lane created for this. PLACEMENT: Epoch 4, same dependency block as C4/C5 -- do not build ahead of the adoption-physics/conversion-window mechanics this depends on for who gets offered what, when.

atoms.atom_name: Payments & billing maturity audit (M2 entry gate) -- 22+ existing modules graded vs D/E charters
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (EPOCH2_RATIFIED.md immediate effects 1+3): M2's entry gate, joins the weekend background work NOW (unlike W5_1's build, which waits on this audit's verdict). Grade all billing/payment modules vs the D/E charters; Expert Hour run under a payments-operations-veteran persona (mandate lifecycle, Bacs cycle timing, failure/representment reality, prepay); classify each module COMPANY-LOGIC (behaviour) vs RAILS-PHYSICS (timing, failure codes, cash arrival); seed the simplifications register. The audit's verdict dictates M2's shape (harden-existing vs rebuild), decided on evidence before spine rewiring -- report in the Monday SPIKE_WEEKEND pack. This atom's own 'level' is the audit process itself reaching a verdict, not a build-capability level -- target=1 reflects 'audit complete, verdict published', not a maturity claim about the audited modules.

2026-07-11 AUDIT COMPLETE, level 0->1 (AT TARGET). Two parallel Expert-Hour forks covered 38 modules total: DD/Bacs-rails cluster (16 modules, docs/design/M2_PAYMENTS_AUDIT_DD_RAILS.md) and billing/collections/arrears cluster (22 modules, docs/design/M2_PAYMENTS_AUDIT_BILLING_COLLECTIONS.md). VERDICT: HARDEN-EXISTING for the company-logic layer -- both audits independently found the same shape, real modules correctly cited against real Ofgem SLCs/PPM Rules 2019/real 2022-23 crisis events, not a naive/fabricated pass. NEW BUILD required for the Banking & Payment Rails simulator (W5_1_banking_payment_rails) -- the Bacs rails-physics layer (3-day submission/processing/settlement cycle, AUDDIS mandate setup, ARUDD failure reason codes, ADDACS amendments) is ENTIRELY ABSENT, not partially modeled; every DD/mandate state transition happens synchronously today. Real gaps found beyond the rails absence: (1) two duplicated-register pairs (direct_debit.py vs dd_mandate_register.py; prepayment.py's inline emergency-credit fields vs ppm_emergency_credit_register.py) needing consolidation before W5 wiring lands; (2) 18 of 22 billing/collections modules (breathing_space_register.py, winter_moratorium.py, disconnection_warning.py, debt_referral.py, annual_statement.py, tariff_variation.py, account_closure.py, billing_dispute.py, renewal_engine.py, switching.py, etc.) are well-built, correctly-cited, but COMPLETELY UNWIRED -- 'paper compliance', correct in isolation, connected to nothing, a real wiring gap not a redesign gap; (3) capacity_to_pay.py recommends PPM conversion for vulnerable fuel-poverty customers, contradicting the real 2023 regulatory direction restricting forced PPM for vulnerable customers -- a genuine domain-sense defect, not yet fixed; (4) switching.py and account_closure.py use date.today() internally instead of an as_of parameter -- the same point-in-time-blindfold anti-pattern class M1 targets, found OUTSIDE the pricing/hedging path it was originally scoped to, registered here rather than fixed opportunistically. loop_stage set to idle: the audit's own job (reach a verdict) is done; the NEXT action (W5 build, register consolidation, the capacity_to_pay/date.today() fixes) belongs to other atoms/a fresh review, not a further action this audit atom itself can take.

atoms.atom_name: Double-entry ledger + management accounts (income statement)
notes: 2026-07-10 FRAME finding: balance_sheet() genuinely balances (equation_holds=True, verified for every real year 2016-2025 in production data) -- the ledger mechanism itself is sound. Real, complete gap found against the real_world_twin (a real supplier's statutory accounts): UK Corporation Tax is entirely absent. NOT simply another margin bug to fix blind -- the EBIT% anchors this session's MARGIN_REALISM work already uses are BY DEFINITION pre-tax, so the pre-tax-only figure is correct for those comparisons. BUILT 2026-07-10 (eighteenth draw): added Corporation Tax as a genuinely NEW, additive triplet (profit_before_tax_gbp/corporation_tax_gbp/profit_for_year_gbp) via a new uk_corporation_tax_gbp() function (real UK rates: 19% flat pre-April 2023, then 19%/25%/marginal-relief via HMRC's own 3/200 Standard Fraction) -- net_margin_gbp itself is completely UNCHANGED in meaning and value (verified with a direct with-year-vs-without-year test), and the new fields are None unless a year is explicitly passed, so every pre-existing call site is unaffected. income_statement.py's docstring documents this is strictly additive. Deliberately did NOT touch the balance sheet (no real anchor for CT payment-timing, ~9 months after period end in reality -- left as a known, honest scope limit rather than invented).

atoms.atom_name: Single reconciled revenue definition across every reporting surface
notes: 2026-07-10 audit: fra_ratio_series was already correct (my prior note was an unverified assumption, corrected); generate_insights.py had a real live instance of the Step 1 bug, fixed. ROOT CAUSE TRACED (third draw, not deferred again): the years[]/ledger net-margin divergence traces to saas/bill_generator.py billing non-commodity cost via a single blended rate (saas/non_commodity.py) while simulation/hedged_settlement.py computes the same cost per-levy/per-period -- two independently-built, never-reconciled models, bidirectional/non-monotonic gap consistent with a real volume/timing mismatch, not a bug in either. Full numerical reconciliation re-scoped to D2_three_clocks (its natural home) rather than resolved here -- E2's OWN scope (gauge/legibility) is now closed on its own terms. depends_on ADDED 2026-07-10 (twelfth draw): formalising what was already true in prose -- E2's remaining gap to level 3 is full numerical reconciliation, which is D2_three_clocks's job, not a further action E2 itself can take. D2 in turn depends on W1_reveal_over_time, now idle pending the just-opened Tier 1 gate (docs/review_gates/POINT_IN_TIME_SNAPSHOT_TIER1.md) -- so this whole chain is honestly, transitively blocked, and the existing depends_on mechanism (not loop_stage=idle) is the structurally correct way to express it, since E2's own loop_stage genuinely IS frame-appropriate work if/when D2 unblocks.

atoms.atom_name: Company-internal authz (roles, maker-checker, segregation of duties)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (director in-console, sandbox/security-profile decision): governance/compliance capability for later epochs -- role-based access control for company-internal decision authority (who/what can approve a pricing action, sign off a large customer refund, etc.), maker-checker separation (the person/process proposing a financial-effect action is never also the sole approver), and segregation of duties across the company's own decision-making functions (distinct from the agent's own harness security profile -- this is the SIMULATED COMPANY's internal governance, a real UK-supplier operational-controls concern, feeding the go-live NFR register's authn/authz category). Registration only, no design or build -- placed at epoch:5 pending real sequencing.

atoms.atom_name: Supervisor self-refill (dial-weighted maturity-map draw, replacing the fragile prose-scan)
notes: v1 wiring lands 2026-07-10 -- this very atom is the one being closed by that wiring. 2026-07-10 HARDEN pass (this atom drew ITSELF, fittingly): adversarially reviewed _maturity_map_draw() before a 4th live incident found the next gap rather than after. Found a real crash risk -- a malformed atom (a hand-editing typo like a quoted level_current string, or an explicit dial_inherited: null) would raise TypeError uncaught, aborting find_work() before it reaches the backlog-prose fallback -- silently reintroducing the exact idle-hole class of bug this mechanism was built to eliminate, specifically during agenda+staging-empty periods (self-refill's own use case). Fixed: per-atom validation now isolates a malformed atom to 'excluded from this draw', not 'the whole draw stops working forever'. Known, accepted lower-severity gap not fixed (self-defeating, not crash-causing): an atom listing itself in its own depends_on would permanently self-exclude rather than crash -- an obvious typo, visually apparent on review, left undefended rather than adding more complexity for a safe-side failure mode. 3 new tests, 452/452 background tests pass, epistemic PASS, live supervisor restarted.

atoms.atom_name: Production-readiness non-functional evidence pass (recoverability, security posture, capacity, availability)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P2, advisor-staged, director-decided -- PRODUCTION_READINESS_BASELINE.md, background-lane, explicitly 'do not displace the spike queue'). No measured baseline currently exists for this business's non-functional properties. Part A scope: recoverability (enumerate everything canonical existing ONLY on Skynet; test an actual restore of one representative artefact -- advisor amendment: to a SCRATCH location, never over live state; report measured RTO/RPO, not assumed), security posture (secrets inventory, exposed-surface inventory, dependency vulnerability scan -- advisor amendment: raw findings route to the PRIVATE ops repo ONLY, the public surface carries a sanitised summary never the map of weaknesses; absorb the harness sprint's existing sandbox/Pattern-C recommendation as an input rather than re-deriving), performance/capacity (measured full-run wall time/memory/disk and scaling with customer count, identify the first resource breaking at 10x/100x population), availability (daemon-stack real uptime from existing logs, every current single point of failure). Non-negotiable: measure, don't assert (R9). Immediate-action carve-out: any live-risk finding (unrecoverable canonical data, exposed credential) gets fixed under normal tiering NOW, not deferred -- the agent reviews and acts on such findings personally rather than delegating remediation, per this session's own consequential-action discipline. Dispatched as a background-lane fork; findings pending.

2026-07-11 PART A COMPLETE, level 0->1. No live risk found matching the imminent-danger tier (no exposed credential value, nothing about to be committed unsafely). Real findings, all measured not asserted: (1) Recoverability -- repo IS GitHub-mirrored (real off-machine copy for tracked content); company/data/*.db (4 SQLite files, largest 2.1MB, the company's own operational financial/customer state) and both .env.* secret files were gitignored with NO upstream copy at all -- matches the 'unrecoverable canonical data' carve-out criteria literally even though not imminent-danger; restore test performed for real (236-byte artefact, scratch location, byte-for-byte verified, ~22ms). ACTED ON per the carve-out: new background/backup_company_data.py backs up all 4 DB files to the private ops repo (verified via md5sum match), wired into process_run_complete.py's cycle so it stays current, not a one-off. (2) Security -- 2/2 secret files confirmed gitignored/600-permissioned/never in git history; zero raw secret-shaped strings elsewhere; 1 medium finding (5 stale unauthenticated dev file-servers running ~2 days, not a data leak); 1 low finding (wide-open CORS on an otherwise-authenticated endpoint); pip-audit not installed, no automated CVE scan possible. Detailed findings routed to the PRIVATE ops repo per the security boundary; public summary sanitised. (3) Performance -- confirmed 19 customers/~470s-run/2.9MB output matches CLAUDE.md's established figure; 2 real O(n²) patterns found by reading simulation/run_phase2b.py directly (per-customer full linear scans of all_records) -- ~100x cost at 10x population, ~10,000x at 100x for that specific pass, code-verified not load-tested; docs/observability/risk-committee-log.md found unbounded at 277MB. (4) Availability -- real crash history in session-watchdog-log.md (2 crash-loop episodes + one ~22min nvm-path outage in the last week); confirmed exactly 6 daemons share the NTFY-env-var SPOF; whole stack single-machine by design, no redundancy. 4 new tests (backup script), 520 background/ tests pass, epistemic PASS. H4_go_live_nfr_register can now carry a real 'current measured state' column from this evidence.

atoms.atom_name: Go-live NFR register (availability/SLOs, security, data protection, operational resilience, capacity, incident response)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P2, advisor-staged, director-decided -- PRODUCTION_READINESS_BASELINE.md). Extends the existing obligations-register pattern to non-functional/operational production requirements: one entry per category (availability/SLOs, latency budgets, security incl. authn/authz/secrets/pen test, data protection incl. GDPR/encryption/backups, operational resilience linking the existing cyber-baseline obligation, change management/rollback, capacity, incident response), each recording what a real UK supplier needs, current measured state (fed by H3_production_readiness_nfr_evidence's Part A), go-live tier (blocker/should-have/post-live), and which epoch it lands in. This register is a direct input to Epoch 5 (go-live analysis), maintained from now -- placed at epoch:5 since that's where it's consumed, though the register itself starts being populated now. Depends on H3's Part A evidence existing before entries can carry a real 'current measured state' column.

atoms.atom_name: Security profiles (Developer/Restricted/Hardened) -- sandbox posture as a director-governed model
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED + PARTIALLY BUILT (director in-console, hybrid sandbox model, absorbing HARNESS_BEST_PRACTICE_ASSESSMENT.md's Pattern-C recommendation as the input rather than re-deriving it). Three profiles: (1) DEVELOPER -- local build lane, current access + 'Option 2 floor' -- secrets genuinely moved out of the working tree to ~/.config/synthetic-enterprise/ (background/secrets_location.py, new-location-first/old-path-fallback during the transition), application-level egress allowlist against the named real endpoints (background/egress_allowlist.py) -- BUILT this session, level 1 reflects this real floor, not the full target. (2) RESTRICTED -- cloud/routine lanes, PR-only, no secrets -- named and registered as what already holds for those lanes (no new build required, matches existing Routines/cloud-environment posture); not yet formally documented as its own named profile. (3) HARDENED (Pattern C+: container, unreadable creds, audit log, RBAC) -- defined now, registered as an EPOCH-5 GO-LIVE NFR BLOCKER (feeds H4_go_live_nfr_register's security category), built and rehearsed before go-live, not built now -- true OS-level network enforcement belongs at this profile's container boundary, not a host-level iptables edit the current harness's own sudo ban correctly prevents. HARD RULE (now in CLAUDE.md): profile changes are director-console-only, the agent can never alter its own profile -- Tier 1, no exceptions, regardless of how reversible a specific change might seem.

2026-07-11 RESTRICTED PROFILE VERIFIED AGAINST LIVE STATE, REAL GAP FOUND + CONTAINED (measure-don't-assert, per the same discipline as the production-readiness audit's stale-dev-servers finding): rather than accept the prior entry's own assumption that Restricted 'already holds' for the cloud/Routines lane, checked the ACTUAL live config via RemoteTrigger get on trig_01Xj4Xkj7sD5Fmv7nZVzW41c ('Daily phase-close health check (pilot)', environment_id=poesys, next_run_at 2026-07-12T03:01 UTC). REAL FINDING: its allowed_tools carried full Bash/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/WebFetch/Tmux/REPL/SendUserFile access plus live Gmail + Spotify MCP connectors wired in -- directly contradicting the director's own explicit binding constraint from the 2026-07-11 05:59 UTC in-console directive ('report-only, issues/PRs, never pushes main, daily schedule max, pause-on-anomaly as kill switch'). This is exactly the anomaly condition that directive named the pause mechanism for, and the director himself designated 'pause' (not a scope edit) as the correct response -- so paused it (RemoteTrigger update, enabled:false, confirmed in the response) rather than attempting to reconfigure its tool/connector scope myself, since deciding what Restricted SHOULD grant is a profile-shaping judgement call reserved for the director under the hard rule above, not a call the agent makes even in the safety-tightening direction. Flagged via [ACTION NEEDED] NTFY (id P1ACEEAYWMba) with the full finding; routine is inert pending director review of intended scope. Level moved 1->2 for the real verification-and-containment work; NOT moved to 3 -- that still requires the director's decision on correct Restricted-profile scope and Restricted being properly documented as its own named, enforced profile (today it exists only as an assumption that turned out to be wrong in practice), which is exactly the class of thing this same finding just proved needs re-deriving from live state rather than re-asserting from the original registration.

2026-07-11 LEVEL 2->3, both named conditions genuinely met (not force-advanced): the director's scope decision arrived via docs/staging/done/ADVISOR_STEER_ROUTINE_SCOPE.md (advisor-staged, self-identifies as relaying a live director decision) -- allowed_tools narrowed to a genuinely minimal read-only set and verified via an independent re-fetch (R1: never trust the update response), gate closed to docs/review_gates/done/. Restricted is now formally documented as an ENFORCED profile, not an assumption: CLAUDE.md's new 'Routine creation, standing rule' (this session) gives it concrete, testable content -- minimal allowed_tools, empty/verified connectors, R1 pre-run verification, disable-rather-than-widen-scope-to-fit -- and the Security-profiles HARD RULE line now cross-references it explicitly instead of leaving Restricted as a bare label. Two honest real findings surfaced along the way, both corrected rather than glossed: MCP connectors could not actually be stripped via the RemoteTrigger API (a real platform limit, not a fix I could complete), but re-checking the ORIGINAL pre-incident config showed those connectors already had permitted_tools:[] throughout -- correcting my own earlier NTFY, which overstated that specific risk; and the routine's own 'issue-only output' requirement turned out structurally unachievable with a genuinely minimal read-only tool set, so per the staged decision's own fallback rule the routine stays disabled rather than forcing a resolution. loop_stage build->harden: the three profiles are now built, tested against a real incident, and documented -- what remains (Hardened's actual container build) is explicitly Epoch-5, out of this atom's scope by its own registration.

atoms.atom_name: Historical data reveal-over-time (point-in-time blindfold at the source, not just company/ code)
notes: 2026-07-10: this atom touches the epistemic law (SIM/company boundary, point-in-time blindfold) -- CLAUDE.md Tier 1, category (a). DISCOVER finding (internal + external research, both landed): sim/risk_engine.py::calculate_sigma_recent() is already self-contained/safe but company/trading/hedge_decision.py::estimate_price_volatility() is caller-trusted, only made safe by an external wrapper at its one known call site (the already-fixed hedge-volatility bug) -- no live second violation, but a template others could copy. TIER 1 GATE CLOSED (director, in-console via staged NTFY): approved as recommended (register, don't build now). PLACEMENT CORRECTION (director): this is Epoch-2 CORE, not a standalone W1-lane item -- the COMPANY-SIDE face of the reveal-over-time spine, sibling to D2_three_clocks (the BILLING-side face), one architecture not two. Sequencing left to the advisor's epoch framing. Interim standing instruction: keep patching individual call sites only if a live bug actually surfaces -- no proactive build before the epoch sequencing names its turn. BOUNDED START APPROVED 2026-07-10 (director NTFY, correlated with from_rich_20260710_203008.md): 'build the ordering-invariant foundations (bitemporal event log + as-of snapshot interface) and the W1/D charters; full campaign sequencing arrives via the advisor's epoch framing this weekend.' Level 0->1 (FRAME->BUILD): company/interfaces/bitemporal_event_log.py (BitemporalEventLog, real dual-time-axis valid_time/transaction_time semantics, 17 tests) + company/interfaces/point_in_time_view.py (PointInTimeView, constructed once per decision, wraps the existing MarketDataPort adapter for price/forward observables plus the new bitemporal log for settlement-restatement-sensitive facts, 12 tests) -- both scoped exactly to the closed gate's own Option A sizing ('1-2 files, a class + a handful of methods'). Near-term detector also built: .claude/hooks/block_point_in_time_read.py (HARNESS_BEST_PRACTICE_ADOPTION.md item 1a, now explicitly director-authorized, flags new company/saas code referencing a full historical dataset with no as_of/bisect bounding evidence). NOT yet done: no existing call site (estimate_price_volatility() etc.) has migrated onto PointInTimeView -- the object exists and is proven correct in isolation, real migration is next, sequencing per the advisor's epoch framing.

2026-07-11 self-referential mechanism bug found and fixed: loop_stage was left at 'build' from the bounded-start work, contradicting this atom's own recorded standing instruction ('no proactive build before the epoch sequencing names its turn') and its idle sibling D2_three_clocks. Consequence was real and repeated: the dial-weighted self-refill selector treats loop_stage=build as actionable, so it re-drew this exact atom on essentially every empty-queue supervisor grant this session (matching the same self-referential-fallback bug class already fixed twice this session in PRIORITIES.md backlog matching and the fingerprint-based stuck-escalation). Corrected to loop_stage=idle to match D2_three_clocks and this atom's own prose -- the field now honestly reflects 'blocked pending the advisor's epoch framing,' not 'ready to build.'

2026-07-11 EPOCH-2 FRAMING RATIFIED, loop_stage restored to build (docs/design/THE_VALUE_CYCLE_FRAMING.md, SPIKE slot 3 = 'M1 at full parallel depth'). Attempted the obvious next step (migrate estimate_price_volatility()'s call site in simulation/run_phase2b.py onto PointInTimeView) and found it would have been WRONG, caught before writing the code (R4 diagnosis discipline): PointInTimeView's market_data_port is backed by tools/market_adapters/frozen_2025.py -> tools/live_market.py, which reads sim/cache/elexon_ssp_full.json -- a frozen snapshot explicitly for the LIVE DECISION path (tools/run_live_decisions.py-style present-day company view; the module's own comment: 'Merged into the LIVE decision path only, here'). The historical BACKTEST simulation's own price records (elec_records/gas_records, what _price_history_as_of() actually bounds) come from a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT pipeline: sim/system_prices_history.py::get_system_prices_range() hits the LIVE Elexon API directly for the run's own date range. Migrating the hedge-decision call site onto PointInTimeView/MarketDataPort as originally planned would have silently swapped the historical backtest's real settlement-price basis for an unrelated frozen 2025-snapshot feed -- corrupting the entire historical simulation, not fixing anything. Also confirmed MarketDataPort has no bulk-history method at all (only single-point spot/forward/summary reads) -- would need extending regardless. REAL NEXT STEP (not yet built): either (a) extend PointInTimeView.get_history_as_known() to be backed by a BitemporalEventLog populated from the historical simulation's OWN elec_records/gas_records (not MarketDataPort), or (b) promote _price_history_as_of()'s already-correct per-call-site bound into a small dedicated as-of-view class wrapping the sim's own record lists directly, never touching MarketDataPort at all. Sizing/choice between (a)/(b) is the next real M1 depth-work session's first task.

2026-07-11 REAL CALL-SITE MIGRATION BUILT, level 1->2. Chose option (a) (one architecture, not two): `market_data_port` made OPTIONAL on PointInTimeView (same fail-loud-if-missing pattern already used for bitemporal_log), new `build_price_bitemporal_log(elec_records, gas_records)` populates a BitemporalEventLog from the historical sim's own records (daily-mean price per commodity/date -- fits the log's one-record-per-valid_time model exactly, since estimate_price_volatility() already aggregates to daily means internally, confirmed by reading its actual code before designing around it), new `PointInTimeView.get_price_history_as_of(commodity)` reconstructs the exact {settlementDate, systemSellPrice} shape the volatility function expects. Both real call sites in simulation/run_phase2b.py migrated (electricity + gas hedge decisions); the old `_price_history_as_of()` wrapper + its cache RETIRED entirely (not left as dead code) -- confirmed zero remaining call-code references (only historical comments). transaction_time==valid_time is a documented simplification (no real settlement-run revision modeling at the price level yet) with an explicit escape hatch: a genuine restatement would `.record()` with a later transaction_time, which `history_as_known_at()` already handles correctly by construction (untested for THIS use case specifically, since no real restatement data exists yet -- exit test's 'cannot see past now' half is now structurally true and tested; the 'restatement versions correctly' half rests on BitemporalEventLog's own existing test coverage, not freshly exercised here). 26 new/changed tests across company/interfaces/test_point_in_time_view.py + tests/simulation/test_run_phase2b.py, full related suites (company/interfaces + company/trading + simulation run_phase2b/hedged_settlement, 779 tests) pass, epistemic PASS (simulation/ importing company/interfaces/ matches the same already-accepted 'layering oddity, not a violation' precedent as W3_1_price_cap_binding). NOT yet done, level 3 still requires: the event-drained loop (sim loop currently steps, not events) and materiality gates + lazy valuation (the affordability mechanism) -- both untouched by this pass.

2026-07-11 DISCOVER+FRAME on the remaining two sub-components (docs/design/M1_EVENT_DRAIN_MATERIALITY_FRAME.md), BUILD deliberately NOT started. Investigated the real code before assuming the framing's abstract language meant a full rewrite: simulation/run_phase2b.py's OUTER decision loop (line ~780-902, all_terms interleaved chronologically then iterated once) is ALREADY event-shaped -- one iteration per real renewal/term event, not customer×tick nesting. The genuine step-based cost is ONE LEVEL DEEPER, in simulation/hedged_settlement.py's run_hedged_term/run_deemed_term/run_flex_term, each doing `for period in range(1,49)` per day -- but this generates REAL UK half-hourly settlement records, the actual ground truth every downstream number (billing, margin, this session's own CT/VaR/forecast-cashflow work) is built from, not wasted overhead a naive 'skip some periods' reading of lazy valuation would suggest. Real runtime today: 468-484s (~8min) for the full 2016-2025 run (docs/observability/sim-runner-log.md, 6 samples) -- not currently blocking anything, a genuine FUTURE scaling concern once M3 (population draws) and M4 (full replay) land, not a live problem. Zero prior art anywhere in the codebase for materiality/lazy-eval concepts -- genuinely greenfield. Deliberately did NOT attempt a same-day rewrite of hedged_settlement.py: it produces literal ground truth, a subtly-wrong change could silently corrupt years of historical data for a problem that isn't urgent (two-way-door filter: don't build against an unresolved design question when nothing forces the decision now). loop_stage set to idle -- genuinely blocked pending a director/advisor call on whether to pre-empt the scaling risk now or defer to when M3/M4 make it load-bearing, not a self-refill-answerable FRAME/BUILD task.

2026-07-11 SEQUENCING RESOLVED (director, in-console): DEFER the settlement-generation rewrite to M4 -- M4's full history rederivation forces the sim through hedged_settlement.py anyway, so rewrite it once there, against a design validated by M2 running on the EXISTING generation first (real load on the current code path, real evidence for the rewrite's shape, rather than guessing now). loop_stage stays idle on this sub-component through M2/M3; picks back up only when M4 starts. FORMAL EXIT TEST RUN same day: THE_VALUE_CYCLE_FRAMING.md's M1 exit test is 'the hedge decision literally cannot see past now; a restatement lands as an event and downstream values version correctly.' Half 1 (cannot see past now) was already covered for the real pathway (test_structurally_excludes_future_dates, company/interfaces/test_point_in_time_view.py). Half 2 (restatement versions correctly) had only ever been exercised generically at the BitemporalEventLog level, never through get_price_history_as_of() -- the actual pathway estimate_price_volatility() consumes -- an explicitly flagged gap in the prior entry above. New test_restatement_versions_correctly_through_price_history added closing that gap: records an initial price, then a later-transaction_time restatement for the same date with a different value; asserts a decision_time before the restatement's transaction_time sees the OLD value and a decision_time after sees the NEW value, both via get_price_history_as_of() itself. Result: PASS -- tests/company/interfaces/test_point_in_time_view.py (25 tests, was 24) + full related suites (company/interfaces + company/trading + simulation run_phase2b + simulation hedged_settlement, 780 tests total) all pass, epistemic verifier PASS (493 files scanned, no violations). Both exit-test halves now structurally true AND tested through the real hedge-decision pathway.

atoms.atom_name: Ofgem price cap as a BINDING constraint on SVT pricing (not just a lookup)
notes: 2026-07-11 BUILT (SPIKE_WEEKEND item 1, MARGIN_REALISM Step 5): found the real gap by reading run_deemed_term() directly -- it computed spot*(1+deemed_premium) with ZERO reference to the Ofgem cap, completely unbound (the existing Phase 47a cap-clamp only ever applied to FIXED-tariff terms, never to deemed/SVT). Real UK deemed/SVT customers cannot legally be charged above the cap regardless of wholesale spot cost -- this is the actual mechanism that squeezed/killed real suppliers buying at spot through the 2021-22 crisis while capped on the sell side. Fixed: billed_rate = min(spot*(1+premium), get_cap_unit_rate_gbp_per_mwh(commodity, year)) for resi customers, 2019+ only (pre-2019 and non-resi stay uncapped, matching the cap's own real scope) -- same clamp convention already used for fixed-tariff terms, extended consistently rather than inventing a new pattern. New `cap_bound` field marks when clamping actually occurred. Margin can now legitimately go negative on a capped settlement period, matching real crisis economics (verified via a dedicated test). Note on architecture: this reuses the existing company/pricing/ofgem_price_cap.py import already present in simulation/run_phase2b.py (Phase 47a precedent) -- a SIM module importing a COMPANY module is a layering oddity (backwards from the SIM being the 'real world' company should discover, not vice versa) but is NOT a point-in-time-blindfold violation (the cap data is static, real, historical, not derived from anything a real company couldn't independently know) -- flagged, not fixed, consistent with the pre-existing precedent this change extends rather than a new decision. 6 new tests (post-2019 clamp, negative-margin-once-capped, pre-2019 uncapped, non-resi uncapped, gas-vs-electricity cap table selection, backward-compatible default). Real before/after effect on the margin trajectory (the 'bridge attribution' SPIKE_WEEKEND also asks for) awaits the next natural sim_runner cycle regenerating run_output_latest.json -- not fabricated from an isolated run under time pressure; to be written up once real post-fix data exists.

2026-07-11 HARDEN sweep Expert Hour run (SPIKE_WEEKEND item 5, energy-market-skeptic-veteran persona, read run_deemed_term() + ofgem_price_cap.py + all 6 cap tests directly): PASS overall (scope, year-boundary handling, commodity selection all correct) with one real gap logged, not silently accepted -- the cap table's ANNUAL granularity doesn't capture the real Ofgem cap's 2-4x/year updates, material specifically in the 2021-22 crisis window this atom targets (e.g. the real ~54% Apr 2022 jump). Recorded in company/compliance/sanity_adjudication.py ledger as expert_hour:W3_1_price_cap_binding (adjudicated-real). Not re-opening level_current (still 2/2, the binding-constraint mechanism itself is correct) -- this is granularity debt on an already-working mechanism, tracked for a future intra-year cap-table pass if crisis-period precision becomes load-bearing.

atoms.atom_name: Typed, versioned-message SIM/company boundary adapters (go-live seam)
notes: forward-preference only, 2026-07-08; already-shipped Phase 3 code not reworked. 2026-07-10 self-refill draw (FRAME-stage): refreshed WALLED_INTERFACES_SKETCH.md's audit -- all 5 candidate flows still accurate against current code, added a 6th (tools/market_data_port.py::MarketDataPort) the original sketch omitted -- already at target shape (typed Protocol + point-in-time-safe via an explicit as_of param), directly cross-referencing the W1_reveal_over_time DISCOVER finding as the natural reference implementation if that finding's structural snapshot object is ever built. The sketch's own Step 2 (meter-reads reference-flow conversion) is classified Tier 3 (reversible, not Tier 1), but PRIORITIES.md carries an existing, explicit standing instruction: 'Awaiting director rank -- do not start the first reference-flow conversion without it.' loop_stage CORRECTED 2026-07-10 (own mistake from the prior draw, caught on the very next draw): set to build reflexively (the next real action IS a build task) without applying the same idle-exclusion logic already built for exactly this situation -- reverted to idle, since 'blocked pending an external director decision' is precisely what idle already means in this schema (W3_price_cap_binding's own precedent), not a new field to invent.

2026-07-11 UNBLOCKED, loop_stage idle->build (verified against real disk state, not assumed -- caught via the W4 charter-writing subagent finding the 'awaiting director rank' note looked stale): the director's own 2026-07-11 05:36 UTC NTFY reply directly addresses this exact block -- 'WALLED_INTERFACES call: agreed -- correct judgement, exactly what the map draw is for' -- confirming his 05:21 UTC calibration correction (asking him to rank this was itself the miscalibration; this class is flag-and-proceed, never a blocking ask) resolves the standing 'awaiting director rank' note. action_needed_register.json's walled-interfaces-priority-rank entry is marked resolved:true, consistent with this. The meter-reads reference-flow conversion (WALLED_INTERFACES_SKETCH.md Step 2) is therefore a real, legitimate, unblocked next build action -- Tier 3, reversible, flag-and-proceed -- eligible for the normal dial-weighted draw to pick up as an actual BUILD phase, not held pending further director input.

2026-07-11 STEP 2 BUILT (via interface-steward subagent, reviewed and committed by me): tools/meter_read_port.py -- MeterReadMessage (frozen, schema-versioned dataclass) + MeterReadPort (runtime_checkable Protocol), same pattern as market_data_port.py/credit_bureau_port.py. Wired live into simulation/run_phase4c_on_phase2b.py's real meter-read call site -- to_log_entry() is a lossless identity round-trip on the pre-conversion dict, so meter_read_log output and every downstream consumer (annual_report, generate_dashboard_data, generate_billing_ledger, population_sanity, sanity_daemon) are byte-for-byte unchanged; genuinely zero blast radius, trivially revertible. Epistemic boundary made explicit at the seam: billed_consumption_kwh (observable) vs true_consumption_kwh (SIM-internal, evidence-only, never company-readable when status=='estimated') -- same pattern as CreditCheckResult.true_creditworthy. 6 new tests + 72 regression (touched + consumer modules) pass, epistemic PASS. level_current STAYS at 2, not advanced to 3: this is one of ~5-6 candidate flows in the sketch's table (meter reads, smart-meter comms, customer contact, acquisition funnel, banking rails -- market data was already done) -- level 3 per this atom's own target means the FULL crossing set typed, not the first proof. loop_stage stays build: 'generalize the pattern' (sketch step 3) to the remaining flows is the real next action, each independently testable/revertible per the sketch's own two-way-door framing.

2026-07-11 SECOND FLOW BUILT, ADDITIVE ONLY -- NOT YET LIVE-WIRED (via interface-steward subagent, reviewed and committed by me): tools/acquisition_funnel_port.py -- FunnelStageMessage + AcquisitionFunnelMessage (frozen, schema-versioned), AcquisitionFunnelPort Protocol, same pattern as the meter-read port. Real finding: the seam is narrower than the internal FunnelStageEvent/AcquisitionFunnelResult dataclasses -- simulation/run_phase2b.py serialises a hand-built dict PROJECTION into acquisition_funnel_log (drops per-stage cost_increment_gbp, adds billing_account from loop context) -- these messages type that actual crossing dict, not the internal object, which is the correct target per the sketch's own framing ('the seam is what saas/company are allowed to see'). Epistemic boundary carried forward: credit_bureau_true_creditworthy (SIM ground truth, evidence-only, mirrors CreditCheckResult.true_creditworthy) vs the observable credit_bureau_passed/score_band; a bureau_divergence property explicitly labelled analytics-only. 7 new tests (built from the real run_acquisition_funnel producer, not synthetic fixtures) + meter-read suite both pass (13 total), epistemic PASS (both diff-mode and full --files scans, 463 files, clean). HONEST GAP, not glossed: unlike meter-reads, this conversion is NOT wired into the real run_phase2b.py call site -- port+tests exist and are proven correct against real producer output, but production code still emits the raw dict directly. level_current STAYS at 2 (not advanced): 'proven in isolation, not yet live' is a materially smaller achievement than the meter-read flow's live wiring, same distinction this project already uses for W1_reveal_over_time's PointInTimeView (L2 = built and proven, L3 = migrated at real call sites). Wiring this port into run_phase2b.py's real call site is the concrete next increment, separate from converting a third flow.

2026-07-11 SECOND FLOW NOW LIVE-WIRED (via interface-steward subagent, reviewed and independently re-verified by me before committing): simulation/run_phase2b.py's real call site now constructs AcquisitionFunnelMessage.from_log_entry(...) and appends .to_log_entry() -- same construct-then-serialise-back pattern as meter-reads, +17/-4 lines, single file. Process note for the record: the subagent stalled once waiting on a ~14min full-sim baseline test instead of using the established fast-deselect pattern (test_main_produces_* tests take ~100min); caught via file-state monitoring (git status showing zero change for an unusually long time) rather than trusting an ambiguous 'still running' self-report, redirected, and it corrected course -- the earlier stall message itself was NOT treated as a real completion despite the harness surfacing it as one, per this project's own 'verify before trusting a self-report' discipline. I independently re-ran the fast test suite myself post-commit-review (111 passed, 3 deselected, matches the agent's own figures exactly) and a full epistemic scan (463 files, PASS) rather than taking the report on trust alone, given this touches simulation/run_phase2b.py directly. Of the sketch's original 6-row table, meter reads and acquisition funnel are now BOTH live-wired (market data and credit bureau were already at target shape before this effort started) -- 4 of 6 flows now genuinely done. level_current STAYS at 2, not 3: smart-meter comms (currently just an implicit penetration-curve lookup, the largest remaining lift) and customer contact in/out (simulation/contact_centre.py's return dict) are the two real, concrete, unconverted flows left -- level 3 means the full set, not 4 of 6.

2026-07-11 THIRD FLOW BUILT + LIVE-WIRED IN ONE PASS (via interface-steward subagent, independently re-verified by me before committing -- 37 tests, full 463-file epistemic scan, both matching the agent's own figures exactly): tools/contact_centre_port.py -- ContactCentreMessage (frozen, schema-versioned) + ContactCentrePort Protocol, wired into simulation/run_phase4c_on_phase2b.py's real call site alongside the meter-read wiring. Real field shape found (again didn't match the sketch's illustrative guess, third time this pattern has held): {customer_id, period_end, channel, first_response_hours, breached_sla}, not the sketch's {channel, direction, customer_id, correlation_id}. Epistemic finding stated plainly rather than invented: UNLIKE the other two flows, there is NO SIM-internal/company-observable split here -- every field is the supplier's own contact-centre operational record (which customers contacted it, its own response time, its own SLA breach), no hidden ground-truth crosses. LEVEL 2->3, loop_stage build->harden: of the sketch's original 6-row table, 5 are now genuinely done (meter reads, acquisition funnel, contact centre all live-wired this session; market data and credit bureau were already at target shape). The 6th, smart-meter comms, is NOT a real data crossing today -- the sketch's own table calls it 'implicit (penetration curve lookup)', meaning there is no discrete SmartMeterServiceRequest/Response object anywhere in the codebase to convert; building one would mean INVENTING new business logic, which directly contradicts this whole atom's own explicit scope limit ('a SHAPE change to already-correct data, not new business logic', restated in every one of this atom's own port docstrings). Registering this honestly rather than either silently claiming it done or leaving it as unexplained debt: level_target=3 is met for every flow that is a real, existing crossing; smart-meter comms would be a NEW atom (create the crossing, then type it) if ever prioritised, not a remaining increment of this one.

atoms.atom_name: Epistemic verifier extended to data-flow/timing violations, not just literal imports
notes: 2026-07-10 (fourteenth draw): this atom's own claim was FALSE. expert_hour had been marked 'passed, findings: []' since seeding, and level_current=2 implied the TOOL itself gained timing-detection capability. Read the entire 202-line tools/epistemic_verifier.py: it contains ONLY literal import-statement regex matching (FORBIDDEN_SOURCES), zero data-flow/timing detection logic -- confirmed unambiguously by this atom's OWN cited evidence: docs/review_gates/done/HEDGE_VOLATILITY_LOOKBACK_FORESIGHT_BUG.md's own contemporaneous text states plainly 'tools/epistemic_verifier.py does not catch this class of bug -- it scans for company/<->simulation.* import violations, not data-flow/timing bugs.' What actually happened was CLAUDE.md's Tier 1 POLICY definition being broadened to explicitly name timing violations as in-scope for classification purposes -- a real, true change -- but I conflated 'the policy now names this' with 'the tool now detects this' when originally seeding this atom. Corrected level_current down to 1 (the real progress: policy broadened, real incident understanding gained) and loop_stage to idle: actually building real detection into tools/epistemic_verifier.py is Tier 1 (CLAUDE.md names 'the epistemic verifier' explicitly as a safety-control-modification category (b)) -- opened docs/review_gates/EPISTEMIC_VERIFIER_TIMING_DETECTION_TIER1.md rather than build it unilaterally.

2026-07-11 GATE RESOLVED, stayed idle (verified against real disk state -- caught via the W4 charter-writing subagent finding this atom's own text stale): the Tier 1 gate closed 2026-07-10, director decision Option B/C -- 'Verifier gate: B/C confirmed -- register + doc-fix, no build; the PreToolUse hook (adoption sprint) is the near-term detector, the as-of snapshot object the permanent fix. Close the gate.' No automated data-flow/timing detection will be built into tools/epistemic_verifier.py -- a deliberate, director-ratified decision, not an open question. Practical detection burden is carried instead by two REAL, separate, already-built mechanisms: .claude/hooks/block_point_in_time_read.py (near-term) and the W1_reveal_over_time PointInTimeView/BitemporalEventLog structural fix (permanent, in progress under W1's own charter). level_target=3 for THIS atom (the tool itself gaining detection capability) is therefore not the actual direction of travel -- the real fix lives in W1, not here -- flagging that level_target may be the wrong measure for this atom's remaining scope rather than silently chasing it; loop_stage correctly stays idle, at rest, nothing further to build here absent a future incident reopening the question.

atoms.atom_name: Banking & Payment Rails simulator (twin: Bacs/bank/acquirer)
notes: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (EPOCH2_RATIFIED.md immediate effect 1): director-ratified NEW W-lane, three amendments to THE_VALUE_CYCLE_FRAMING.md (banking-as-swappable-interface worry). Built adapter-SHAPED behind an interface mirroring the real protocol -- submission windows, multi-day cycle, AUDDIS-style mandate setup, ARUDD-style failure returns with reason codes, ADDACS amendments; cash lands when the rails say, making the banked clock (D2_three_clocks/C2) honest -- 'rails ARE late-arriving truth, a limb of the spine, not an adjacent system.' Full typed/versioned hardening of this seam is Epoch-3 work (added to the wall's adapter inventory). loop_stage=idle: M2 build (this atom included) starts only after the M2 entry-gate payments-maturity-audit verdict AND M1's exit test both land -- not before, per EPOCH2_RATIFIED.md immediate effect 4.

2026-07-11 'RAILS SIM FIRST' BUILT, level 0->1 (advisor steer ADVISOR_STEER_M2_OPEN.md: 'audit reporting unlocks M2 BUILD... Open M2 build: rails sim first'). WebSearch-verified real Bacs mechanics before building, not assumed: 3-working-day processing cycle (Pay.UK's own Bacs System Principles guide); AUDDIS confirms/rejects mandate setup on Day 2; ARUDD reports a failed collection up to 2 working days AFTER collection day, never instantly (the actual 'cash lands when the rails say' physics); real reason codes (ARUDD 0='Refer to Payer' -- the real dominant insufficient-funds-class code -- 1/2/3; AUDDIS 1/2/3/5), sourced from AccessPaySuite/Hafiz Didarali Bacs reason-code references. Deliberately does NOT duplicate the existing calibrated behavioural failure-probability model (simulation/arrears_engine.py::payment_outcome(), stress-tier-anchored, R13-compliant) -- this module only adds realistic TIMING and REASON CODES around an already-decided success/failed outcome, structurally guarded by a test proving the module contains no probability constants of its own. Reason-code selection is deterministic to the documented dominant code, NOT rng-randomised across the full code set -- no sourced real-world frequency split between codes exists, and fabricating one would make rare codes (Payer Deceased) appear unrealistically often. NOT yet done: wiring this into the live company/billing/direct_debit.py collection flow (still synchronous/instant today) -- a separate, larger integration step, per the framing's own sequencing ('rails sim first, THEN estimated billing -> catch-up rebilling -> settlement timetable -> DD cash engine'). 21 new tests, epistemic PASS (pure stdlib module, zero company/saas coupling).

2026-07-11 RAILS TIMING WIRED INTO THE LIVE DD FLOW, level 1->2 (director in-console approval: 'M2 next step approved: wire rails timing into the live DD flow'). Real finding before building (R4): grepped the whole codebase for company/billing/direct_debit.py's DirectDebitBook/record_attempt/DDPaymentAttempt and found ZERO callers anywhere -- entirely unwired, the same 'paper compliance' class the M2 audit already named for 18 OTHER billing/collections modules. The genuinely live DD-relevant flow is simulation/arrears_engine.py::payment_outcome(), called from compute_emergent_bad_debt()/compute_debt_recovery() -- but those compute REAL ground-truth bad-debt/recovery figures flowing into net_margin_gbp/treasury_cash_balance_gbp, exactly the class of code M1's own precedent already established extreme caution around; a same-day timing change there would shift which calendar year a write-off lands in, silently altering historical financial figures. Deliberately NOT touched. Instead built new simulation/dd_collection_book.py: populates the previously-unwired DirectDebitBook with REAL Bacs-timed collection records, using the SAME unchanged payment_outcome() decision (identical RNG seed and call sequence as compute_emergent_bad_debt()) layered with bacs_rails.py's realistic timing/reason codes -- a new, real, company-observable artefact where none existed before, zero existing numbers changed (confirmed via git status: arrears_engine.py and direct_debit.py both untouched, import-only integration). Caught and fixed a real bug before it shipped: initially shared one rng between payment_outcome() and resolve_submission()'s lag-day draw, which would have desynced every outcome after the first resolved bill from the real ground truth -- fixed with a separate, independently-seeded rng for the rails-timing layer, proven by a dedicated regression test replaying the exact same payment_outcome() sequence independently and asserting an exact match. 9 new tests, epistemic PASS, full arrears_engine test suite (74 tests) still green.
